# E2E Test Overview

This document provides an overview of all end-to-end test cases.
It is generated from the test source code using Ginkgo's outline
feature, which parses test structure without executing tests.

## BareMetalHost bootMACAddress workflows [bmh-bootmac]

File: `test/e2e/bmh_test.go`

### With empty bootMACAddress

1. Creates BMH without bootMACAddress with interface labels
   - Creating BMC secret and BMH with empty bootMACAddress and boot-interface label
   - Verifying BMH was created with empty bootMACAddress
1. Simulates hardware inspection populating NIC details
   - Setting BMH status with hardware details and Available state
   - Verifying hardware details were set
   - Verifying bootMACAddress is still empty before allocation
1. Verifies BMH is ready for allocation
   - Verifying BMH has all required fields and is ready for allocation
   - Verifying boot-interface label maps to correct NIC in hardware details

### With bootMACAddress set but no boot-interface label

1. Creates BMH with bootMACAddress but without boot-interface label
   - Creating BMC secret and BMH with bootMACAddress set but no boot-interface label
   - Verifying BMH was created with bootMACAddress set
1. Simulates hardware inspection populating NIC details
   - Setting BMH status with hardware details and Available state
   - Verifying hardware details were set
   - Verifying bootMACAddress remains unchanged after inspection
1. Verifies BMH is ready for allocation without boot-interface label
   - Verifying BMH has bootMACAddress set and is ready for allocation
   - Verifying boot interface in hardware details matches bootMACAddress

## MNO Day2 Hardware Configuration test [mno-day2-hw-updates]

File: `test/e2e/mno_hw_configuration_test.go`

- Creating namespaces
- Creating ClusterTemplate, HardwareProfiles, and supporting resources
- Creating 11 BMHs with BMC secrets, HardwareData, HFS, and HFC
- Waiting for all 11 BMHs to be visible via List
- Waiting for ClusterTemplate reconciliation
- Creating ProvisioningRequest with v1 hwProfiles (basic BIOS settings)
- Waiting for NAR creation
- Waiting for all 11 AllocatedNodes to be created
- Waiting for day0 to complete (NAR Provisioned=True)
- Waiting for PR HardwareProvisioned=True
- Simulating AllocatedNodeHostMap on PR
- Deleting created resources

### Performs day2 hardware configuration update successfully

1. Should update the PR with new hwProfiles and trigger configuration update
1. Should detect HW configuration changes and begin update process (NAR InProgress=True)
1. Should PR reach InProgress
1. Should complete rolling update: masters first (maxUnavailable=1), then workers (maxUnavailable=3)
   - Polling and advancing BMH state transitions for each node
   - Waiting for NAR to reach ConfigApplied
1. Should PR reach HardwareConfigured=True

### Handles day2 hardware configuration update with BMH error

1. Should update the PR with v3 worker profile (masters unchanged)
1. Should detect worker configuration changes and begin update (NAR InProgress)
1. Should PR reach InProgress
1. Should fail when one worker BMH enters error state during update
   - Finding the first in-progress worker
   - Failing the worker BMH
   - Waiting for the worker node to reach Failed
   - Waiting for NAR to reach Configured=False/Failed
1. Should PR reach HardwareConfigured=Failed

### Handles mid-flight profile change by abandoning stale updates

1. Should update PR with v1 worker profile requiring BIOS and firmware changes
1. Should have 3 workers in ConfigUpdate and advance one BMH to Servicing
   - Detect worker configuration changes and begin update (NAR InProgress)
   - Waiting for at least 3 workers in ConfigUpdate with v1 profile
   - Picking one worker and advancing its BMH to Servicing with config-in-progress
1. Should change to v2 worker profile mid-flight, defer abandon for Servicing worker, then converge all
   - Changing worker profile to v2 while v1 updates are in-flight
   - Waiting for 7 workers to converge to v2 while Servicing worker defers abandon
   - Verifying the Servicing worker is still in ConfigUpdate with v1 profile (deferred abandon)
   - Transitioning the Servicing BMH to OK to allow deferred abandon
   - Waiting for NAR to reach ConfigApplied after all workers converge
   - Verifying all 8 worker nodes converged to v2 profile
1. Should PR reach HardwareConfigured=True

## SNO End-to-end ProvisioningRequestReconcile with hardware manager [sno-provisioning]

File: `test/e2e/sno_provisioning_test.go`

- Creating namespaces, ConfigMaps, and ClusterImageSet
- Creating ClusterTemplates
- Creating BMH pool namespace, HardwareProfile, and 4 BMHs with HardwareData
- Updating BMH status with hardware details and setting Available state
- Waiting for ClusterTemplate reconciliation
- Deleting created resources

1. Verify status conditions if ClusterInstance rendering fails
   - Creating ProvisioningRequest with incomplete ClusterTemplate
   - Waiting for validation and render failure conditions
1. Starts hardware provisioning
   - Creating ProvisioningRequest with complete ClusterTemplate
   - Waiting for hardware provisioning to start
1. Creates NodeAllocationRequest and AllocatedNode
   - Waiting for NodeAllocationRequest to be created
   - Waiting for AllocatedNode to be created by hardware manager
1. Updates the BMHs with the right labels
   - Verifying bmh-2 was selected over bmh-4 despite identical criteria
   - Verifying non-selected BMHs do not have allocation labels
1. NodeAllocationRequest and AllocatedNode complete
   - Verifying FirmwareUpdateNeeded annotation on selected BMH
   - Transitioning BMH to Preparing state
   - Waiting for config-in-progress annotation on AllocatedNode
   - Updating HostFirmwareComponents status to match HardwareProfile
   - Creating PreprovisioningImage and transitioning BMH to Available
   - Waiting for NAR and AllocatedNode to reach Provisioned=True
1. Completes hardware provisioning
   - Waiting for HardwareProvisioned condition to become True
1. Skips re-rendering the ClusterInstance when configuration changes occur during active provisioning
   - Waiting for PR to move past hardware provisioning to cluster installation
   - Changing template version to trigger re-render attempt
   - Verifying ClusterInstance is not re-rendered during active provisioning
